content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :  File (84200 and 84211)                                Date  :  March 4, 1981


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Rich Eichman                                            A-81-03-013

Subject:  Campaign Disclosure


At the opinion request meeting on February 26, 1981, the following
issues were confirmed:

     (1)  An ongoing slate committee that supports a slate of
          different candidates for the same local office is
          not considered a general purpose committee, and there-
          fore must file on the election schedule during each
          election period with the local filing officers (the
          committee always files under Section D of the campaign
          manual).

     (2)  For the purpose of reporting expenditures on Schedule
          E or F:

          (a)  The cumulative amount begins with the first day
               of the period covered by each campaign statement.

          (b)  Each expenditure of $100 or more must be disclosed
               in detail.  (i.e. If payments were made to the
               same payee for $125 and $75 during the reporting
               period, only the payment of $125 need be itemized
               on the campaign statement).

          (c)  If the expenditure is a contribution, the following
               information must be included:

               (i)   Candidate - full name of candidate, district
                     and office sought and amount of contribution;

               (ii)  Measure - number or letter of measure, juris-
                     diction of measure and amount of contribution.

               The cumulative amount per candidate or measure need
               not be disclosed on Schedule E or F; the cumulative
               amount will be disclosed on the Allocation Schedule.

sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses campaign disclosure requirements for slate committees supporting different candidates for the same local office, and specifies itemization thresholds and requirements for reporting expenditures on campaign statements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Slate committees are not general purpose committees and must file on the election schedule with local filing officers. Expenditures of $100 or more must be disclosed in detail, with cumulative amounts calculated per reporting period. Contributions must include candidate or measure information with corresponding amounts, though not all contributions need separate itemization if below threshold.

No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; information extracted from body text discussing opinion request meeting. File references suggest this relates to campaign finance committees (files 84200 and 84211). High confidence in document type and general topic, lower confidence in specific details due to text quality.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The document addresses campaign disclosure requirements for slate committees supporting different candidates for the same local office, and specifies itemization thresholds and requirements for reporting expenditures on campaign statements.

CONCLUSION: Slate committees are not general purpose committees and must file on the election schedule with local filing officers. Expenditures of $100 or more must be disclosed in detail, with cumulative amounts calculated per reporting period. Contributions must include candidate or measure information with corresponding amounts, though not all contributions need separate itemization if below threshold.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : File (84200 and 84211) Date : March 4, 1981 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Rich Eichman A-81-03-013 Subject: Campaign Disclosure At the opinion request meeting on February 26, 1981, the following issues were confirmed: (1) An ongoing slate committee that supports a slate of different candidates for the same local office is not considered a general purpose committee, and there- fore must file on the election schedule during each election period with the local filing officers (the committee always files under Section D of the campaign manual). (2) For the purpose of reporting expenditures on Schedule E or F: (a) The cumulative amount begins with the first day of the period covered by each campaign statement. (b) Each expenditure of $100 or more must be disclosed in detail. (i.e. If payments were made to the same payee for $125 and $75 during the reporting period, only the payment of $125 need be itemized on the campaign statement). (c) If the expenditure is a contribution, the following information must be included: (i) Candidate - full name of candidate, district and office sought and amount of contribution; (ii) Measure - number or letter of measure, juris- diction of measure and amount of contribution. The cumulative amount per candidate or measure need not be disclosed on Schedule E or F; the cumulative amount will be disclosed on the Allocation Schedule.
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter addressing campaign disclosure requirements for slate committees, including filing obligations, expenditure itemization thresholds of $100, and detailed reporting requirements for contributions to candidates and measures.
